change: Add a new grade G8 for logistics team leads at 22.40/hour, on the standard pension scheme.
interpretation: The grade table WS-GRADE-TABLE currently holds 7 entries (G1-G7) with hardcoded rates. To add G8 at $22.40/hr, we extend the table to 8 entries, add a new initialization filler for G8, and adjust the search loop in 300-GROSS. The pension logic (paragraph 550-PENSION) already applies the standard scheme to any grade not explicitly listed as G6 or G7, so G8 will automatically receive the standard 5% employee / 3% employer contribution, consistent with the requirement. No change to tax, NI, or other deductions is necessary.

blastRadius.why: The grade rate table definition (OCCURS 7), its initialization data, and the search loop that iterates over grades must all be extended to accommodate G8 and return its hourly rate.

evidence.quote: 01 WS-GRADE-TABLE. ... 05 WS-GRADE-ENTRY OCCURS 7 TIMES. ... 05 FILLER PIC X(6) VALUE 'G71995'.

blastRadius.why: The pension paragraph explicitly lists G6/G7 for the legacy scheme; G8 will fall into the ELSE clause and receive standard contributions. No code change required, but behavior must be confirmed to match the 'standard pension scheme' requirement.

evidence.quote: IF EMP-GRADE = 'G6' OR EMP-GRADE = 'G7' ... ELSE ... WS-PEN-EE-STD ...

plan.detail: Change OCCURS 7 TIMES to OCCURS 8 TIMES on the WS-GRADE-ENTRY definition.
Insert a new line: 05 FILLER PIC X(6) VALUE 'G82240'. This sets G8 rate to 22.40 (integer 2240 cents).
Change 'PERFORM VARYING WS-IX FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL WS-IX > 7' to '... UNTIL WS-IX > 8'.
Recompile the program; no copybook changes needed, so no rebind of PAYRPT04 or extract job is required. The EMP-GRADE field (PIC X(2)) already accommodates G8.

Run existing regression tests covering grade lookup, gross pay, pension contributions, and payslip production. Verify that a timecard with a G8 employee calculates correctly: hourly rate 22.40, no legacy pension, standard auto-enrolment contributions.

risks: The OCCURS count and loop bound must be changed in unison; forgetting either leads to a table-bound error or failure to locate G8.
No other business rule is affected, but the hardcoded rate structure remains frozen at 2019 values for existing grades; future rate adjustments still require source edits.
Ensure that the new FILLER string matches the existing packed format (6 bytes, no implied decimal). A misplaced space or digit would silently corrupt the rate for G8.

estimate.legacyWay: A payroll specialist would need to locate the grade table, understand the filler encoding, modify OCCURS and loop, recompile, and test pension logic – estimated 2-4 hours of focused work.
estimate.withStrata: Using this dossier, a developer can execute the changes in 15-20 minutes; the verification of pension logic adds 10 minutes – total ~30 minutes.
